title: Map View on items view center
excerpt: It would be nice if we can get a map inside of the items view, so i can click on an item and pull it up exactly on the map.

posts.author: apantoja (Archie Pantoja)
posts.cookedHtml: It would be nice if we can get a map inside of the items view, so i can click on an item and pull it up exactly on the map.

posts.author: MCautreels (Maarten Cautreels - Tryve)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Archie,

I know your original post is from a while back but we’ve recently added a new feature to our monday Marketplace app Location Toolkit: Map Picker!

This adds an item view which allows you to select a location on a map in the first place but it also shows you the selected/configured location on a map just like this:

Screenshot 2024-06-25 at 22.56.591920×1100 224 KB

Best,

Maarten

posts.author: Daminaio (dectatang)
posts.cookedHtml: Integrating a map into your items view can be a great way to improve usability. There are two main approaches to consider. An inline map with popups displays a small map snippet for each item, allowing quick location checks without leaving the list. Clicking the item opens a detailed map and information popup. Alternatively, a linked map view keeps the list format but adds map icons next to each item. Clicking the icon or the item itself switches to a dedicated map showing all items as markers. This provides a broader location overview. The best option depends on your application’s context and the level of detail you want to show. Consider factors like map app provider integration, performance, and user experience. A well-designed map integration can significantly enhance how users interact with your items by offering a clear visual representation of their locations.
